name: Sopa de Salchichón
intro: Sopa de salchichón is a smoky, salty soup made with salami and fideo, cut angel hair noodles that add bulk and soak up the flavor of the seasoned broth. It is saucy, meaty and a warm hug in a bowl, since it also contains potatoes and calabaza squash. My mom makes this soup a lot during the cold season in New York City—coming home to a bowl filled me with joy and love. It’s so good, I always have seconds.

ingredients.item: package Beyond Meat sausage sliced into ½-inch (1.3-cm) disks
chopped fresh cilantro, plus more leaves for garnish
Salt and freshly ground black pepper (optional)

method: In a large soup pot over medium-high heat, combine the olive oil, Beyond Meat sausage and onion. Cook for 4 minutes, stirring so the meat doesn’t stick and the onion does not overcook. Stir in the garlic and cook until fragrant, for less than a minute.

Stir in the sofrito, tomato sauce, liquid smoke, stock, Sazón and adobo, and cook for 2 minutes while stirring. Add the olives, corn, potato, cilantro and oregano, and boil for 15 minutes, or until the potato is tender. Add the calabaza and fideo and cook until the noodles are tender. Taste and add salt and pepper, if you wish.
Serve the soup in a bowl and garnish with fresh cilantro; eat with a side of white rice or avocado.
